text: In software development, Continuous Integration (CI) is a practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each integration is verified by an automated build system that compiles the code. Following this, a series of automated tests run to check for errors. If a test fails, the team is immediately alerted. A typical CI pipeline will include these steps in sequence, allowing issues to be caught early before they become bigger problems. This makes the development process faster and more reliable. With CI, teams spend less time fixing bugs and more time building new features, ensuring stable and high-quality software.
